data.description:
## Why
Serial wall-clock is ~N×CI for N ready stacks; batching collapses it to ~1 CI cycle, which is the wall-clock-optimal option under the Free-tier GHA concurrency cap (~3 concurrent runs) where speculation would just queue.
## What
Assemble the queued `Autosubmit` changes/stacks into ONE combined relation chain on current `main`, trigger CI ONCE on the combined tip, and land them together atomically if green (Graphite/bors stack-aware model on Gerrit relation chains).
## Scope
Phase 2. The batching assembly + single-run + atomic land. Verified-casting and batch-failure handling are separate stories. Depends on Phase 1.
## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Assembles queued changes/stacks into one combined linear chain on the current `main` tip.
- [ ] Triggers exactly ONE CI run on the combined tip (not one per change).
- [ ] On green, submits the whole batch atomically (ancestor-atomic submit); the tested tip == the landed tree.
- [ ] Batch size bounded to one CI run's job budget (fits the GHA concurrency cap); documents the bound.
data.title: S7 Cross-stack batching: combined-chain CI on tip, land together (Phase 2)
